description:
Create a 3D solar model from an aerial photograph.

Instructions:
1. Open SketchUp Make 2017.
2. Import the aerial image located at 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\site_aerial.jpg' onto the ground plane (File > Import, ensure 'Use as Image' is selected).
3. Use the Tape Measure tool to scale the model. Identify the main horizontal roof ridge of the house in the image, click both ends of the ridge, type '12m' (12 meters), and press Enter to resize the model.
4. Use the drawing tools (Line/Rectangle) to trace the footprint of the main building directly over the image.
5. Use the Push/Pull tool to extrude the traced footprint upwards by exactly 4 meters to create a 3D building.
6. Select the top roof face of your 3D building and use the Skelion plugin to insert a solar panel array (use default panel settings in landscape orientation).
7. Save the completed model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\satellite_model.skp'.
